headline: Small SSE windswell, glassy dawn — a log session before the onshore fills in.
today: Waist-high at best on a short-ish 5-6s SSE, fair angle, so nothing to write home about. The play is early: dawn is offshore and the morning goes light and glassy before the S onshore shows up around midday and mushes it out. Grab the log, ride the incoming tide toward that 1:19p high if you can beat the wind, otherwise it's a skip.

daysToWatch.note: Swell builds but SE cross-on wind runs 17-18mph all day — junky and bumpy, not worth much.
Chest-high but from due S at a marginal angle with WSW cross wind. Size without shape.
Best of the week here. Chest-high SSE at 6s with N offshore dawn through morning — clean window, bring the groveler or fish early.
